_about: SOURCE OF TRUTH for the fak TURN-TAX efficiency curves (visuals/60-hero-turntax-curves.svg), rendered by tools/hero_turntax_gen.py. fak's analog of the MiniMax 'GQA vs MSA' 3-panel attention-efficiency chart: a baseline whose cost rises ~linearly vs a fak line that stays ~flat, the gap filled, one big multiplier per panel. The shape is real — re-prefill cost is linear in context/fleet, resident+addressable KV is not. Each panel names its OWN baseline honestly: a structural per-cold-miss tax (panel 1), a MODELED WebVoyager elimination vs the naive floor (panel 2), and the conservative-vs-tuned-SOTA fleet number (panel 3). Every number traces to fak/BENCHMARK-AUTHORITY.md / docs/webbench-baselines.md by commit. Edit this file, then run `python tools/hero_turntax_gen.py`.

meta.title: The turn-tax: re-prefill cost is linear, resident KV is not

headline: Three lenses on one structural win — the work that never gets done twice
subhead: An agent that drops its KV re-prefills the shared setup on every cold miss; fak keeps it resident and addressable, so the cost stays flat as context, fleet, and turns grow.

panels.title: Per-turn prefill cost
panels.subtitle: vs context length · baseline = re-prefill on a cold KV miss
panels.mult_foot: 2,560× already at a 128k agent context — the per-cold-miss tax = P / R

panels.title: Fleet prefill, WebVoyager
panels.subtitle: vs worker count · baseline = naive re-prefill · MODELED geometry, 643 tasks
panels.mult_foot: 8.8× at a single worker vs the naive floor — modeled geometry, no wall-clock

panels.title: 50-turn × 5-agent fleet
panels.subtitle: vs turns elapsed · baseline = a tuned warm-cache SOTA stack
panels.mult_sub: less serving work, vs tuned SOTA
panels.mult_foot: the conservative number — vs the naive re-prefill loop it is ~60×, which we don't lead with

footer: Apple M3 Pro · app_version 0.30.0. Panels 1–2 baseline against re-prefill (the standard non-resident path every cold miss pays); panel 3 against a tuned warm-cache SOTA stack. fak/BENCHMARK-AUTHORITY.md · docs/webbench-baselines.md.
